data.title: Quanta Services is top pick in infrastructure services: Piper Sandler
data.description:
Piper Sandler on Monday started research coverage on a select group of specialty infrastructure service companies, citing long-term tailwinds from electrification, renewables and data center expansion. Despite near-term uncertainties following recent policy shifts, the firm is bullish on sector leaders, naming Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) its top pick.

Kashy Harrison, senior research analyst at Piper Sandler, initiated coverage on Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR), MasTec (NYSE:MTZ), Primoris Services (NYSE:PRIM) with Overweight ratings, and MYR Group (NASDAQ:MYRG) with a Neutral rating.

“We believe these specialty contractors are benefiting from key infrastructure development trends including: electrification, load growth, rising renewable penetration, aging infrastructure and an aging utility workforce,” Harrison wrote.

Resilient business models

Harrison estimated that among transmission, distribution, generation, data centers and telecom, the companies are tapping into a $200 billion-plus annual market opportunity, which can support durable earnings growth despite cyclical risks. The group delivered a compound annual growth rate of 5% in their earnings per share from 2020 to 2024. Piper Sandler forecasts 9% EPS growth in 2025.

Importantly, the firms under coverage have business models built for resilience, with many contracts structured to pass inflationary costs on to customers and backlogs spanning multiple quarters that provide visibility into 2025 earnings.

“Leverage toward utility capex, contracts designed to pass equipment inflation to customers, and robust backlogs with lengthy construction cycles should provide insulation for 2025,” Harrison noted.

'Liberation Day' ushers in new risks

Still, the outlook isn’t without caution. Harrison acknowledged that the geopolitical and economic uncertainty introduced by “Liberation Day” — the date President Trump introduced sweeping new tariffs — has created a fog over project timelines and capital decisions.

“Everything changed on 'Liberation Day' and the ramifications are developing,” he wrote. “Uncertainty is inconducive to project FIDs [final investment decisions], thereby elevating the risk of delayed nearer-term bookings that could impact 2026 and beyond.”

While 2025 earnings appear relatively secure, Piper warns that revenue visibility beyond that becomes increasingly cloudy as clients reassess budgets in response to tariff pressures and rising costs.

PWR: Proven compounder in volatile market

Among the group, Quanta Services (PWR) stands out for its consistent execution and market leadership, according to Piper Sandler.

“Quanta Services (PWR) has met or exceeded quarterly street EPS expectations every quarter since 3Q'19,” Harrison said, pointing to the company’s track record. “It is the market leader in T&D [transmission and distribution] and renewable generation services, with potential upside from electrical system work for data centers and onshoring of manufacturing supply chains via the Cupertino acquisition.”

Piper Sandler views Quanta Services (PWR) as a blue-chip compounder with room to grow, even in a volatile macro environment.

Outlook: Favoring execution and stability

As Harrison summed up, in a post-tariff world defined by political uncertainty and cautious capital spending, Piper Sandler is tilting toward operators with proven execution, strong contracts and strategic positioning in future-facing infrastructure.

“In turbulent times, we’re biased toward best-in-class operators with proven track records of execution,” Harrison concluded. “Accordingly, Quanta Services (PWR) is our relative favorite.”

data.title: Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca get EU approval for Datroway
data.description:
• Daiichi Sankyo announced on Monday it received approval in European Union for its DXd antibody-drug conjugate Datroway for the treatment of HR+, HER2- breast cancer.
  • Datroway is a TROP-2 directed drug discovered by Daiichi Sankyo and being jointly developed and commercialized by Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN).
  • The company  said that the drug was approved for the treatment of adults with HR+, HER2- unresectable or metastatic breast cancer after prior chemotherapy.

data.title: Wix.com climbs after upgrade to Equal Weight by Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wix.com (NASDAQ:WIX) shares climbed 4% during Tuesday morning market action after the web development company was upgraded to Equal Weight from Underweight by the analysts at Wells Fargo.

With Wix.com shares declining 31% year to date, Wells Fargo finds that any risks are now effectively "priced in."

"We are still skeptical of the FY25 +13-15% y/y CC bookings guide, but investor convos suggest this risk is well understood & F/X is now less of a drag," said Wells Fargo analyst Alec Brondolo, in an investor note. "Since 4Q EPS, we est F/X has improved from a 2% FY25 bookings drag to 1%; while we still believe a CC accel from 1Q to 2Q on a 5pts tougher comp is unlikely, factoring in the F/X change, consensus bookings appear more achievable. Additionally, investor convos suggest a high degree of skepticism on the FY25 accel, suggesting risk is priced in."

Wells Fargo estimates that 20% of Wix.com revenue is derived from e-commerce customers, and the business mix leans towards services, which provides some insulation from tariffs.

"High business mix toward events/services positions WIX well relative to risk introduced by tariffs," Brondolo noted.

Wells Fargo also increased its price target on the stock to $176 from $169.

The bank said it needs to see more stabilization from the company before upgrading again to Overweight.

Last month, Wix.com was initiated with a Sector Outperform rating by Scotia Bank. 

"Ultimately, we think that improving growth from AI innovation, as well as compounding subscription growth and increased operating leverage, should drive [free cash flow per share] generation," analyst Nat Schindler wrote in a note to clients.

data.title: Micron to pass on tariff-related surcharge on some products to customers - report
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) has informed U.S. customers that it plans to impose a surcharge on some products from April 9, to account for new U.S. tariffs unveiled by President Donald Trump last week, Reuters reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Shares of Micron rose about 5% on Tuesday.

The memory chipmaker told its customers in a letter that while the tariffs' exempted semiconductors, which form a part of Micron's portfolio, the levies applied to memory modules and solid-state drives, or SSDs, the report added.

Those products, which are used to store data in several products from cars to laptops and data center servers, would now see a surcharge, the report noted.

The company's manufacturing facilities located abroad are largely based in Asia, including China, Taiwan, Japan, Malaysia and Singapore.

Micron did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The letter to customers echoes comments by Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra on the company's earnings' call on March 21, when he said that, "where tariffs do have an impact, we intend to pass those costs along to our customers."

U.S. customs agents sarted collecting Trump's unilateral 10% tariff on all imports from several nations on Saturday. Higher "reciprocal" tariff rates of 11% to 50% on individual countries are slated to take effect on Wednesday at 12:01 a.m. EDT, the report noted.

Chip stocks have faced heat since Trump's' tariffs were announced on trading partners, including China and Taiwan, despite semiconductors being listed among imports exempted from the tariffs.

Micron also makes High Bandwidth Memory, or HBM, chips, the market for which is led by SK hynix, followed by compatriot Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF).

data.title: Justice Department said to shutter crypto unit amid Trump's deregulation push
data.description:
The U.S. Justice Department recently notified employees that the regulator was scrapping a unit focused on crypto enforcement, aligning with the Trump administration's push for deregulation, according to a Tuesday media report that cited a memo.

The National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Unit was disbanded "effective immediately," U.S. Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche wrote in the four-page memo, as quoted by Fortune.

He added that the DOJ is "not a digital assets regulator." "However, the prior Administration used the Justice Department to pursue a reckless strategy of regulation by prosecution.”

The move marks the President Donald Trump administration's latest attempt to shift toward lighter regulatory oversight of crypto. He has already signed executive orders to create a national bitcoin (BTC-USD) reserve using seized bitcoins, for instance, and also created a working group to come up with a comprehensive regulatory framework for digital assets. 

Meanwhile, Blanche told DOJ staff to start targeting its crypto-related prosecutions and probes toward individuals who exploit digital asset investors, Fortune reported, while not pursuing cases against crypto exchanges, mixers and offline wallets. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has already dropped a barrage of enforcement actions against crypto firms, including crypto exchange Coinbase (COIN) and crypto mixer Tornado Cash.

In Tuesday morning trading, crypto prices pared some of their recent losses as global risk assets rebounded as hopes grew that the U.S. might enter into trade negotiations with some countries. The two largest tokens by market cap -- bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD) -- gained a little over 1% each over the last 24 hours.

It's worth noting that, despite the many crypto-friendly measures implemented this year by the government, bitcoin (BTC-USD) has come under pressure amid heightened economic uncertainty from growth fears stemming from tariff policies.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow turn green as hopes for trade negotiations rise
data.description:
Wall Street major averages advance on Tuesday, as tariff-driven volatility appeared to ease and hopes grew that the U.S. might enter into trade negotiations with some countries.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +3.3%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +3.6%, and the Dow (DJI) was +3.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.25%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 6 basis points to 3.84%.

U.S. stocks dropped again during Monday's trading session as ongoing tariff concerns continued to fuel uncertainty and rattle the broader market averages.

"Yet despite this morning’s recovery, markets are hardly in a good place right now, with an incredible amount of volatility still happening across different asset classes," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday his administration won't pump the brakes on imposing tariffs on products coming into the U.S., but he’s open to discussing trade deals with other countries.

"Admittedly, Trump did sound open to reaching deals with other countries, saying in his message on China tariffs that 'Negotiations with other countries, which have also requested meetings, will begin taking place immediately,'" Allen added. 

On late Monday, Trump also said the European Union will have to commit to buying $350 billion of American energy to get a reprieve from the sweeping tariffs.

"Trump threatened further tax increases on U.S. consumers, with an additional 50% tax on imports from China. Rerouting supply chains becomes a viable avoidance mechanism at such levels. Japan is the first major country to negotiate with the U.S. That prioritization feels like a throwback to the trading environment of the 1980s," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

 Vietnam said it will increase its purchases of American goods, including defense and security products, as part of an effort to address its significant trade surplus with the U.S. and also requested a 45-day delay in the imposition of 46% U.S. tariffs.

data.title: HUMBL announces name change application and JV with MultiCortex
data.description:
• HUMBL (OTCPK:HMBL) said on Tuesday that it will be submitting an application to the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) to change its corporate name to HUMBL Ventures. 
  • The company has a deadline of June 30, 2025, to apply to change the legal name from HUMBL to HUMBL Ventures. 
  • The company also announced today a joint venture with MultiCortex AI, a U.S. and Brazilian-based artificial intelligence company, as the newest addition to its holding company portfolio.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, HUMBL will hold a 51% equity stake in the joint venture, while MultiCortex co-founders Bruno Ghizoni and Alessandro Faria will retain 49% and lead daily operations.
  • HUMBL will invest up to $3 million USD from its upcoming Regulation A+ public offering to support go-to-market initiatives and expansion.

data.title: Iron Mountain trades higher as Truist initiates coverage with Buy rating
data.description:
Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) was trading higher as Truist Securities initiated coverage of the stock with a Buy rating and $95 price target.

The stock was 2.44% up Tuesday pre-market to $79.42.

"In our view, the company is poised to drive ~10% annual AFFO/share growth over the next three years, driven by a growing data center franchise that should emerge as a primary driver of AFFO/share growth and a catalyst for SOTP multiple expansion," said a team of analysts led by Tobey Sommer.

"We think the risk/reward has improved, with shares off 39% from peak on tariff-fueled mkt decline, reversing AI trade, overblown gov't headwinds and capital intensity vs. 51% in the GFC," said the analysts in a research note.

The data center operator and provider of document storage services gets a Buy rating on average from the sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors. However, the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Strong Sell.

Quant assigns IRM a score of 1.46 on a scale of 5, with D+ for Profitability and Momentum and C- for Valuation.

data.title: Glassdoor Employee Confidence Index edges up in March
data.description:
Employee confidence ticked up in March from a record low in February, according to the Glassdoor Employee Confidence Index released on Tuesday.

The percentage of employees reporting a positive six-month business outlook increased to 45.2% in March from 44.7% in February. The prior month's reading was revised up from 44.4%.

"Economic uncertainty remains a significant drag on the sentiment of workers as tariffs, federal funding and workforce cuts, and general business uncertainty disrupt investment and hiring plans," Glassdoor said. That sentiment may slump again after President Trump announced sweeping tariffs against almost every country last week.

The industries with the largest declines in employee confidence are all vulnerable to government layoffs and funding cuts — aerospace & defense (-11 percentage points Y/Y); government & public administration (-6.6 ppts); and nonprofit & nongovernmental organizations (-4.6 ppts).

data.title: RTX's Pratt & Whitney develops additive manufacturing to reduce engine repair time
data.description:
RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX) +3.2% pre-market Tuesday after saying its Pratt & Whitney subsidiary has developed a new additive manufacturing repair process for critical geared turbofan engine components that will reduce process time by more than 60%.

The company said it is working to industrialize the repair, which will then be scaled and applied throughout the global GTF maintenance, repair and overhaul network.

Pratt & Whitney is currently navigating an issue with the GTF engines and is conducting an inspection drive for potentially flawed components, which has led to the grounding of hundreds of planes in recent months.

The additive repair process will help improve turnaround time and bring down tooling costs, according to Kevin Kirkpatrick, Pratt & Whitney's VP of Aftermarket Operations at Pratt & Whitney: "A more agile, additive repair process allows us to better serve our customers by improving turnaround time, while reducing tooling costs, complexity and set up. At the same time, it reduces our dependency on current material supply constraints."

Pratt & Whitney expects to recover $100M worth of parts over the next five years through the implementation of additive repairs throughout its MRO process.

Separately, RTX (NYSE:RTX) announced agreements with MTU Aero Engines and Delta Tech Ops to increase capacity in its MRO services.
